items.title: $7K worth of Labubu dolls stolen from Los Angeles store, authorities say
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>A group of masked thieves stole about $7,000 worth of Labubu dolls from a Los Angeles-area store this week, authorities said.
The incident took place early Wednesday morning at a store in La Puente, a city about 18 miles (29 kilometers) east of Los Angeles, the LA County Sheriff's Department said. The department said the suspects used a stolen Toyota Tacoma in the incident, which was recovered shortly afterward.

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Thousands march in Messina to protest construction of bridge linking Sicily with Italian mainland
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Thousands of people marched on Saturday in the Sicilian city of Messina to protest against the construction of the world’s largest suspension bridge, which would link the Italian mainland with Sicily in a massive 13.5-billion-euro ($15.5 billion) infrastructure project.

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

items.title: With 'Black Star,' Amaarae finds the joy: 'This is the most fun I've had' | AP interview
items.author: Associated Press
items.content: <article>Amaarae's "Black Star" is rooted in identity, lyricism and self-expression. The Ghanaian-American artist sat down with Associated Press entertainment journalist Liam McEwan to reflect on finding the joy in completing her third album, why living far from home has strengthened her connection to her roots, how a comment from her younger brother reshaped her approach to songwriting — and the lasting piece of advice from her father that continues to guide her music. #amaarae</article>

items.title: Astronauts return to Earth with SpaceX after 5 months at the International Space Station
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Four astronauts are back on Earth after a five-month stay at the International Space Station. Their SpaceX capsule parachuted into the Pacific off the Southern California coast on Saturday, a day after departing the orbiting lab.

items.title: Heatwave worsens living conditions of thousands of displaced people in Gaza
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Thousands of displaced people living in tents and among the rubble of destroyed buildings are struggling not only with a lack of food and water, but also with a heatwave that is making their living conditions even harsher.

items.title: London police arrest 150 as pro-Palestinian protesters defy new law
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Thousands marched through the streets of central London Saturday to protest against the ongoing war in Gaza. The protest, organised by the Palestine Solidarity Campaign, called for an end to Israel's offensive in Gaza.

items.title: AP Top 25 Preseason Poll Arrives Monday
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The AP Top 25 preseason poll drops Monday, Aug. 11. First launched in 1936, the poll will be joined by a live blog with analysis, a Q&A, and ways for you to join the conversation as college football season kicks off.
#top25 #collegefootball #shorts</article>

items.title: Chinese lantern festival lights up Philadelphia
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>From vivid marine life to traditional dragons, the skies of Philadelphia are being lit up in magical colours for the annual Chinese Lantern Festival.

Now in its eighth year, the festival has become a seasonal staple for locals and tourists.

#shorts #philadelphia #lanterns #festival</article>

items.title: Wildfire threatens homes southeast of Athens as strong winds hamper response
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>A fast-moving wildfire on Friday forced a series of evacuations southeast of the capital Athens.The blaze approached residential areas as firefighters battled strong winds. (AP Video by Srdjan Nedeljkovic; Production by Derek Gatopoulos and Theodora Tongas)
